claims.claim_text: Kustomize has been built into kubectl since Kubernetes v1.14, via `kubectl kustomize` and `kubectl apply -k`.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters / Did You Know #1
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes Kustomize task page directly states that kubectl has supported kustomization files since 1.14 and shows the exact commands.

claims.claim_text: A Kustomize package is identified by a directory containing `kustomization.yaml`, and `kubectl kustomize DIR` builds from that directory.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 2 / Build a Clean Base
claims.rationale: The kubectl kustomize reference explicitly says the directory argument must contain `kustomization.yaml`.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize renders ordinary Kubernetes resources by applying transformations, patches, and generators before kubectl submits the result to the API server.
claims.rationale: The Kustomize task page describes Kustomize as customizing Kubernetes objects through a kustomization file and shows build/apply flow through kubectl.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize patch matching uses the original resource identity, so a patch should target `webapp` rather than the prefixed rendered name `prod-webapp`.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 3 / production overlay patch note
claims.rationale: The Kustomize docs say patch target matching uses kind/name identity from the patch and separately document namePrefix as a later transformation.

claims.claim_text: The `labels` transformer can include or exclude selectors, while older `commonLabels` behavior applies labels to selectors.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 3 and Part 4 / label transformer discussion
claims.rationale: The Kustomize docs show `labels` with `includeSelectors` and list `commonLabels` as applying labels to resources and selectors.

claims.claim_text: A Deployment selector must match the pod template labels and is immutable after creation in `apps/v1`.
claims.rationale: The Deployment docs directly state both the selector/template-label matching requirement and selector immutability.

claims.claim_text: Namespace scoping applies only to namespaced objects, not cluster-scoped resources.
claims.rationale: The namespaces documentation explicitly distinguishes namespaced objects from cluster-wide objects.

claims.claim_text: Strategic merge patches merge container lists by container `name`, so a wrong container name can add another container entry instead of modifying the existing one.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 5 / Strategic merge paragraph and container-name warning
claims.rationale: The kubectl patch task shows that container lists use strategic merge with `name` as the merge key and demonstrates list-merging behavior.

claims.claim_text: JSON 6902 patches are ordered lists of operations applied to JSON paths.
claims.rationale: RFC 6902 is the primary standard defining JSON Patch documents as ordered sequences of operations over JSON locations.

claims.claim_text: Generated ConfigMaps and Secrets receive a content-hash suffix by default, and `generatorOptions.disableNameSuffixHash: true` disables that suffix.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 6 / generator behavior and disabling suffix
claims.rationale: The Kustomize docs explicitly describe the default content-hash suffix behavior and the `disableNameSuffixHash` option.

claims.claim_text: When a Deployment refers to a generated ConfigMap by its logical generator name, Kustomize rewrites that reference to the generated rendered name.
claims.rationale: The Kustomize docs directly say to reference the logical name and that Kustomize will replace it with the generated name.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl diff -k` compares live configuration against the configuration as it would look if applied from a kustomization directory.
claims.rationale: The kubectl diff reference defines diff exactly in those terms and documents `-k/--kustomize`.

claims.claim_text: Argo CD renders a repo path with Kustomize when that path contains `kustomization.yaml`, and using an overlay path is a supported pattern.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 8 / GitOps controllers paragraph and Did You Know #2
claims.rationale: The Argo CD Kustomize guide directly says Argo renders with Kustomize when the path contains `kustomization.yaml` and says to point path to the overlay.

claims.claim_text: Flux Kustomizations build manifests from `.spec.path`, can set or override namespace, validate objects, and apply them to the cluster.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 8 / GitOps controllers paragraph and Did You Know #2
claims.rationale: The Flux Kustomization documentation directly describes build-from-path, namespace handling, validation, and apply behavior.

claims.claim_text: Kustomize can render Helm charts through the Helm chart inflator via `--enable-helm`.
claims.rationale: The kubectl kustomize reference documents the `--enable-helm` flag for the Helm chart inflator generator.

claims.claim_text: Helm charts use Go templates, values files, and declared chart dependencies.
claims.span_hint: section: Part 8 / Helm comparison paragraphs and table
claims.rationale: The Helm charts documentation directly covers Go-template rendering, values files, and chart dependencies.

claims.claim_text: The module names specific Kustomize task shapes as things the CKA may ask you to do.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, final paragraph
claims.anchor_text: You may be asked to apply a provided overlay, fix a broken `kustomization.yaml`, preview rendered output, or patch a Deployment without modifying the base.
claims.suggested_rewrite: In hands-on Kubernetes work, you may need to apply a provided overlay, fix a broken `kustomization.yaml`, preview rendered output, or patch a Deployment without modifying the base.
claims.rationale: The official exam blueprint is broader than this exact task list, so the sentence should lose its exam-specific framing.

claims.claim_text: The module makes a specific claim about the CKA shell environment starting without aliases.
claims.anchor_text: The CKA environment may also start from a clean shell, so this module uses the full `kubectl` command everywhere.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Timed or unfamiliar shell environments may not include your usual aliases, so this module uses the full `kubectl` command everywhere.
claims.rationale: This is plausible operational advice, but the sentence asserts an exam-environment fact more specifically than the cited sources support.

further_reading.title: Declarative Management of Kubernetes Objects Using Kustomize
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream walkthrough for Kustomize structure, generators, patches, and transformers.

further_reading.title: kubectl kustomize Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful when you want the exact CLI behavior and flags for rendering kustomizations.

further_reading.title: Argo CD Kustomize Guide
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows how Kustomize overlays are used directly in a real GitOps controller.

further_reading.title: Flux Kustomizations
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows the controller-side model for building, validating, and applying Kustomize paths continuously.

notes: No shared citation pool exists yet for k8s/cka/part1-cluster-architecture, so this seed uses per-claim URLs. The strongest dedupe candidates for a future pool are the upstream Kustomize task page, Deployment docs, Argo CD Kustomize guide, and Flux Kustomization docs.
